summary:
Vertebral arteries traverse part but not entirely through the foramen magnum; thus, option A is excluded based on partial passage only. Spinal branches like those of the vagus nerve or spinal cord fully transmit via the foramen magnum without any ambiguity regarding exclusion criteria provided here. To directly address your request while adhering strictly to two concise statements focused solely upon clinically pertinent information related specifically towards answering "foramen magnum transmissions," please consider these revised summary lines below instead which better aligns precisely per instructions given earlier:

The foramen magnum primarily allows transmission of structures such as the brainstem and spinal cord completely passing through its entirety whereas other options including components of the vertebral arterial system have mixed involvement crossing into yet exiting before full transit occurs making definitive exclusions challenging especially when considering complete pathways versus mere entry/exit presence alone. Given strict binary inclusion/exclusion requirements implied herein focusing purely medically functional rather than anatomical extent definitions, final determination hinges critically between clear vs unclear contributions leading ultimately toward excluding vertebral elements due insufficiently meeting 'entirety criterion'. 

In conclusion, after refining focus explicitly onto core medical functionality aspects required accurately reflecting precise nature intended query posed initially about absolute content passages necessitating whole structure completude inside bony canal itself thereby eliminating ambiguous cases leaving us firmly concluding that among listed possibilities only vertebral-related items fail requisite stringent test ensuring total containment necessary fulfilling requirement stated originally asking “transmit ALL EXCEPT”.
